title: B1 — Extend fabricConfigSchema with 10 new tunables for import/archive/review pagination + thresholds
description: Add 10 optional numeric tunables to fabricConfigSchema (import_since_months, import_max_commits, import_cap_archive_candidates, import_cap_review_candidates, archive_lookback_count, archive_max_candidates, archive_min_threshold, review_pending_page_size, review_max_modify_followups, plan_context_max_relevance_paths). All optional+default; do NOT add .strict(). Add corresponding entries to FABRIC_CONFIG_DEFAULTS dict in init.ts and h3 sections in docs/configuration.md.

files.change: Add 10 optional z.number().int().positive().default(<n>).optional() fields following existing pattern (multi-line comment + rc-version provenance); preserve lenient base (no .strict())
Add 10 default entries matching schema defaults; keep comment style consistent with existing entries
Add 10 h3 sections (### <key> (default <value>)) following existing template — rationale + cheat-sheet + rc-version footer
Add 10 default-application tests + 10 explicit-value tests + back-compat test (.fabric/fabric-config.json 7-key minimal still parses)

implementation: Define 10 tunables (recommended defaults — see decision matrix in planning-context.md): import_since_months=2, import_max_commits=200, import_cap_archive_candidates=10, import_cap_review_candidates=50, archive_lookback_count=8, archive_max_candidates=20, archive_min_threshold=10, review_pending_page_size=8, review_max_modify_followups=14, plan_context_max_relevance_paths=64
Add each as optional + .default(<n>) zod field with multi-line `// rc.9+: <purpose>` comment matching existing pattern
Sync FABRIC_CONFIG_DEFAULTS dict in init.ts — every schema field needs a literal-value entry
Document each in docs/configuration.md with h3 + rationale + cheat-sheet (when to raise/lower) + rc-version footer
Add fabric-config.test.ts coverage: default-when-absent (10 tests) + accepts-explicit-value (10 tests) + 7-key minimal config still parses (1 regression test)

reference.pattern: Additive-optional schema extension; triple-binding (schema + defaults dict + docs)
reference.examples: Existing 14 tunables in fabricConfigSchema all follow this exact triple-binding pattern; user's 7-key minimal config at .fabric/fabric-config.json proves additive expansion is safe

criteria: fabricConfigSchema gains 10 new optional+default fields, all numeric.int.positive
Schema parse of .fabric/fabric-config.json (user's 7-key minimal) succeeds and resolves 10 new fields to their defaults
FABRIC_CONFIG_DEFAULTS dict has 10 new keys with values matching schema defaults exactly
docs/configuration.md has 10 new h3 sections; all link back to schema names
fabric-config.test.ts has 20+ new tests; all pass
fabric-config root schema does NOT use .strict() (verified via test that unknown keys silently drop)

rationale.chosen_approach: Additive-optional with explicit defaults; preserves back-compat with all existing fabric-config.json files
alternatives_considered: Group tunables into nested objects (e.g. fabricConfig.import.maxCommits)
Add .strict() to fabric-config root for typo-safety
decision_factors: User's existing 7-key minimal config must continue to parse
Flat namespace consistent with existing 14 tunables
rationale.tradeoffs: Flat namespace gets crowded with each rc.X; nested grouping would be cleaner but breaks existing readers — accepted

manual_checks: Run `cat .fabric/fabric-config.json` to confirm user's 7-key minimal config
Run `fab doctor` and verify no schema parse errors
Run `fab init` in a fresh tmpdir and verify new defaults applied
success_metrics: 0 schema parse errors on existing user configs
All 10 tunables have triple-binding (schema + dict + docs)
